project.name: TrendingFounder
project.description: Discover trending domains globally via Cloudflare Radar, deduplicate them, enrich with a local LLM, and triage in a mobile-friendly Streamlit dashboard.

nodes.name: client.py
nodes.summary: Asynchronous HTTP client for Cloudflare API with configurable rate-limiting and retry logic.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses httpx.AsyncClient with custom retry and rate-limit header parsing.

nodes.name: radar_service.py
nodes.summary: Cloudflare Radar API service that fetches geo-locations and top domain rankings by country.

nodes.name: schemas.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic models for Cloudflare Radar API responses covering geolocations, ranking entries, categories, and metadata.

nodes.name: orchestrator.py
nodes.summary: Core crawl orchestration engine coordinating Cloudflare Radar, LLM enrichment, deduplication, and scoring across countries.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy asyncio with Semaphore, Lock, gather, and dynamic concurrency per country.

nodes.name: progress.py
nodes.summary: Crawl run lifecycle management: creating/resuming daily runs, querying completed countries, formatting progress.

nodes.name: run_daily.py
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for daily crawl with argument parsing, async setup, and graceful shutdown.

nodes.name: dedupe.py
nodes.summary: Domain deduplication pipeline: normalizes, upserts new domains, inserts observations while tracking new vs existing.

nodes.name: lmstudio_client.py
nodes.summary: Async client for LM Studio local inference server with retry, JSON schema enforcement, and domain enrichment.
nodes.languageNotes: Retry with backoff, markdown fence stripping, Pydantic validation of LLM responses.

nodes.name: prompts.py
nodes.summary: Builds structured enrichment prompts for LLM domain analysis combining ranking context, categories, and homepage snippets.

nodes.name: schemas.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for LLM enrichment results with validated summary, category, business model, and scoring fields.

nodes.name: _is_retryable
nodes.summary: Determines if an HTTP exception is retryable (429 or 5xx).

nodes.name: load_existing_domains
nodes.summary: Loads existing domains into a lookup set keyed by normalized domain.

nodes.name: get_or_create_today_run
nodes.summary: Retrieves or creates today crawl run, handling resume of incomplete runs.

nodes.name: get_completed_countries
nodes.summary: Queries country statuses and returns completed country codes.

nodes.name: format_progress
nodes.summary: Formats a crawl run into human-readable progress summary.

nodes.name: parse_args
nodes.summary: Parses CLI arguments for the daily crawl command.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Async entry point initializing services and executing the crawl.

nodes.name: dedupe_and_insert
nodes.summary: Normalizes domain, upserts if new, inserts observation with score.

nodes.name: _is_retryable_lmstudio_error
nodes.summary: Checks if LM Studio error is eligible for retry.

nodes.name: _retry_after_seconds
nodes.summary: Extracts retry-after header with clamped bounds.

nodes.name: _strip_json_fence
nodes.summary: Strips markdown fences from LLM responses.

nodes.name: build_enrichment_prompt
nodes.summary: Assembles structured LLM prompt with ranking context and categories.

nodes.name: CloudflareAPIError
nodes.summary: Custom exception for Cloudflare API errors.

nodes.name: CloudflareClient
nodes.summary: Async HTTP client with rate-limit awareness and retry.

nodes.name: RadarService
nodes.summary: Service layer for Cloudflare Radar geo-location and ranking endpoints.

nodes.name: GeoParent
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for a geographic parent region.

nodes.name: Geolocation
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for a geographic location with coordinates.

nodes.name: Category
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for a ranking category.

nodes.name: RankingEntry
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for a domain ranking entry.

nodes.name: RankingMeta
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for ranking metadata.

nodes.name: CrawlOrchestrator
nodes.summary: Orchestrates crawl pipeline: fetch, deduplicate, enrich, score, persist.

nodes.name: LMStudioClient
nodes.summary: Async LLM client with JSON-schema calls, retry, enrichment pipeline.

nodes.name: LLMEnrichmentResult
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for LLM enrichment output with scoring validation.

nodes.name: logging.py
nodes.summary: Centralized logging configuration using loguru with console and file handlers from settings.

nodes.name: client.py
nodes.summary: GitHub Search API client providing repository topic search with automatic pagination, rate-limit detection, and structured error types.

nodes.name: GitHubAPIError
nodes.summary: Custom exception raised when the GitHub Search API returns an error or an invalid payload.

nodes.name: GitHubRateLimitError
nodes.summary: Exception raised specifically when GitHub rate-limits the search request, carrying the reset epoch when available.

nodes.name: GitHubSearchPage
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass holding a page of GitHubRepoSnapshot items and an incomplete_results flag.

nodes.name: GitHubSearchClient
nodes.summary: Main search client that fetches topic-scoped repositories via the GitHub Search API, handling pagination across multiple pages and rate-limit detection.

nodes.summary: GitHub opencode topic crawler that fetches repository snapshots, diffs against existing data, and persists observations and crawl summaries.

nodes.name: GitHubCrawlSummary
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass summarizing a crawl run with run_id, status, fetched count, new count, and baseline count.

nodes.name: GitHubOpencodeCrawler
nodes.summary: Orchestrator that manages the full crawl lifecycle: creating crawl runs, fetching via GitHubSearchClient, inserting or updating repositories, building observations, and handling failures.

nodes.summary: CLI entry point for the GitHub opencode crawler with argument parsing for topic and limit overrides and inline progress reporting.

nodes.name: parse_args
nodes.summary: Parses command-line arguments for topic name and target repository limit using argparse.

nodes.name: cli
nodes.summary: Main CLI function that creates a GitHubOpencodeCrawler, executes the crawl, and prints the summary fields to stdout.

nodes.name: schemas.py
nodes.summary: Data model defining GitHubRepoSnapshot, a normalized repository snapshot with factory method for API response parsing and method for database row conversion.

nodes.name: GitHubRepoSnapshot
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass representing a GitHub repository with 13 fields, plus from_api_item classmethod and repository_row method for DB persistence.

nodes.name: test_github_discovery.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive test suite for the GitHub discovery pipeline covering baseline runs, incremental updates, error handling, progress reporting, search client behavior, and UI query exclusions.

nodes.name: FakeSearchClient
nodes.summary: Mock search client that returns a predefined list of repositories or raises a configurable error.

nodes.name: FakeGitHubRepository

nodes.name: FakeHTTPClient

nodes.name: normalize.py
nodes.summary: Provides domain name normalization utilities — extracting registrable domains from URLs, building display URLs, and checking against the known-giants list for penalty application.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses tldextract for public-suffix-aware domain extraction.

nodes.name: normalize_domain
nodes.summary: Extracts the registrable domain from any raw URL or domain string, handling www/mobile subdomains, trailing paths, and international suffixes via tldextract.

nodes.name: build_display_url
nodes.summary: Prepends https:// prefix to a normalized domain for consistent display formatting.

nodes.name: is_known_giant
nodes.summary: Checks whether a normalized domain matches the known-giants set for penalty application, performing a lazy import of constants.

nodes.name: scoring.py
nodes.summary: Implements the trending-founders observation scoring system with an 11-factor formula covering rank, novelty, multi-country presence, LLM potential, and penalty adjustments. Core algorithm behind domain ranking.

nodes.name: ScoreBreakdown
nodes.summary: Data class holding individual score components (base, rank, novelty, etc.) with a computed total property and a details dictionary method for debugging transparency.

nodes.name: score_observation
nodes.summary: Computes the composite observation score from 10 weighted factors, applying rank bonuses, category bonuses, novelty boosts, LLM potential scaling, known-giant penalty, and already-reviewed penalty.

nodes.summary: Comprehensive test suite for domain normalization covering URL parsing, casing, subdomain stripping, international TLDs, and error-handling edge cases.

nodes.summary: Tests the normalize_domain function across 15 scenarios including plain domains, HTTPS, paths, subdomains, co.uk/com.br TLDs, and invalid-input error cases.

nodes.name: TestBuildDisplayUrl
nodes.summary: Verifies build_display_url prepends https:// to normalized domains.

nodes.name: TestIsKnownGiant
nodes.summary: Validates is_known_giant against known giants (google.com, youtube.com) and unknown domains, including case-sensitivity behavior.

nodes.name: test_scoring.py
nodes.summary: Thorough test suite for the scoring module covering every score factor: base, ranking type bonus, rank bonus tiers, percentage rank change, multi-country bonus, category bonus, novelty, LLM potential, known-giant penalty, and already-reviewed penalty.

nodes.name: TestScoreBreakdown
nodes.summary: Tests the ScoreBreakdown data class, verifying total aggregation and the details() method.

nodes.name: TestScoreObservation
nodes.summary: Tests score_observation across all scoring factors with individual test cases for each component and a comprehensive full-calculation integration test.

nodes.name: 001_core.sql
nodes.summary: Core schema defining 5 domain tracking tables (domains, crawl_runs, domain_observations, crawl_country_status, domain_comments) with foreign keys, indexes, and audit timestamps.

nodes.name: domains
nodes.summary: Primary table for unique normalized domains with LLM enrichment fields, scoring, review status, and UUID primary key.

nodes.name: crawl_runs
nodes.summary: Tracks each scheduled crawl execution with per-run statistics for countries, requests, new domains, and LLM processing counts.

nodes.name: domain_observations
nodes.summary: Records per-domain appearances in country/day/ranking-type combinations with rank, score change, categories, and raw payload.

nodes.name: crawl_country_status
nodes.summary: Granular per-country progress tracking for each crawl run with status, timing, and item counts.

nodes.name: domain_comments
nodes.summary: Simple user notes table for attaching comments to domains during manual review.

nodes.name: 002_views.sql
nodes.summary: Reporting views (v_domains_today, v_domains_this_week, v_crawl_stats, v_crawl_country_progress) and a paginated RPC function for domain browsing with filtering and sorting.

nodes.name: v_domains_today
nodes.summary: View joining domains with today's observations showing best scores, country coverage, ranking types, and comment count.

nodes.name: v_domains_this_week
nodes.summary: View aggregating domain performance over 7 days with first/last seen tracking and time-bound observation counts.

nodes.name: v_crawl_stats
nodes.summary: View aggregating daily crawl run metrics including new domains, observations, reviews, and high-score counts for the dashboard.

nodes.name: v_crawl_country_progress
nodes.summary: View showing per-country status, timing, and item counts for the latest current-date crawl run.

nodes.name: 003_rls.sql
nodes.summary: Enables Row-Level Security and creates full-access policies on all 5 core tables for internal service-role usage.

nodes.name: 004_opportunity_scores.sql
nodes.summary: Adds LLM-driven Romanian market opportunity scoring columns (opportunity_score, trend_score, confidence, category) to the domains table.

nodes.name: 005_github_opencode.sql
nodes.summary: Schema for GitHub topic discovery providing tables (github_repo_crawl_runs, github_repositories, github_repo_observations) with RLS policies and indexes for the opencode repository feed.

nodes.name: github_repo_crawl_runs
nodes.summary: Tracks GitHub topic crawl executions with source URL, topic, target limits, and fetched/new/baseline counts.

nodes.name: github_repositories
nodes.summary: Stores discovered GitHub repositories with metadata, star/fork counts, review status, and baseline-or-new classification.

nodes.name: github_repo_observations
nodes.summary: Records per-crawl-run snapshots of GitHub repository metrics: rank, stars, forks, and open issues.

nodes.name: 006_optimize_domain_query.sql
nodes.summary: Performance migration adding composite indexes and an optimized get_domains_for_range function with additional enrichment fields.

nodes.name: 007_allow_multiple_crawl_runs_per_day.sql
nodes.summary: Removes the unique run_date constraint from crawl_runs and adds an index to support multiple scheduled crawls per calendar day.

nodes.name: 008_latest_crawl_run_views.sql
nodes.summary: Replaces v_crawl_stats and v_crawl_country_progress views to show only the latest crawl run when multiple executions exist per date.

nodes.name: v_crawl_stats
nodes.summary: Replacement view for v_crawl_stats using DISTINCT ON to return the latest run per date for dashboard metrics.

nodes.name: v_crawl_country_progress
nodes.summary: Replacement view for v_crawl_country_progress scoped to the latest current-date crawl run.

layers.name: UI Layer
layers.description: Streamlit multi-page dashboard with components for domain tables, filters, metrics cards, comments, and data loading for the Today/Week/Stats pages.

layers.name: Service Layer
layers.description: Core business logic including crawl orchestration, domain deduplication and scoring, opportunity scoring engine, CLI dispatch, and shell entry-point scripts.

layers.name: Integration Layer
layers.description: External API clients for Cloudflare Radar, LM Studio LLM inference, GitHub Search topic discovery, and GitHub Actions workflow dispatch.

layers.name: Database Layer
layers.description: Supabase client, async HTTP client, repository classes, SQL query constants, and all SQL schema definitions and migrations.

layers.name: Configuration Layer
layers.description: Pydantic settings, domain constants, environment template, Python project configuration, and IDE/editor settings.

layers.name: Infrastructure & CI/CD
layers.description: GitHub Actions CI/CD workflows for scheduled crawling and debugging, plus VS Code devcontainer configuration.

layers.name: Utility Layer
layers.description: Cross-cutting logging configuration and the understand-anything tool ignore file.

layers.name: Documentation Layer
layers.description: Project README, CLAUDE.md agent guide, design specifications, architecture docs, runbook, changelog, ADRs, implementation plans, and D2 architecture diagrams.

layers.name: Test Layer
layers.description: Comprehensive pytest test suite covering Cloudflare client, Radar service, schemas, crawler, domain normalization/dedup/scoring, LLM enrichment, opportunity scoring, GitHub discovery, database repositories, data loader, UI metrics, settings, constants, and CLI argument parsing.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with README.md to understand TrendingFounder's purpose: discovering trending domains globally via Cloudflare Radar, deduplicating them, enriching with a local LLM, and triaging in a fast mobile-friendly Streamlit dashboard. This document provides the quick-start guide, project structure reference, system architecture overview of the crawl pipeline and opportunity scoring algorithm, and the MVP roadmap — giving you a high-level map before any code.

tour.title: System Architecture
tour.description: The Architecture document provides the system-wide view with eight diagrams covering high-level system flow, data flow, component/module relationships, crawl pipeline stages, database schema, external service integrations, and error handling. Use this as a reference while stepping through the code — it shows how the UI layer (Streamlit), service layer (Python), and integration layer (Cloudflare, LLM, GitHub) connect at a glance.

tour.title: CLI Entry Point
tour.description: main.py is the application's CLI dispatch point, routing to subcommands like update-opportunity-scores and crawl-github-opencode via lazy imports. pyproject.toml defines the project metadata, dependencies, and registers main.py as a console_scripts entry point. Together they establish how the project is installed, built, and invoked — the starting point for understanding how the system is packaged and launched.
tour.languageLesson: Python pyproject.toml centralizes build system config, dependency management, and tool settings (ruff, pytest). The console_scripts entry point registers main:main as a CLI command, making it callable as `trendingfounder` after pip install.

tour.title: Streamlit Dashboard Shell
tour.description: app/streamlit_app.py is the UI entry point — the highest-fan-out file in the project (115 edges). It defines the Streamlit application shell with theming, navbar, pagination, and three page sections: collected data triage, GitHub opencode repository feed, and crawl reports. Every major feature of the dashboard originates from this file, making it the natural starting point for understanding the UI.

tour.title: Dashboard Components
tour.description: Three UI components render the Streamlit dashboard's interactive surface. domain_table.py renders the main results table with status actions, comment display, and detail expanders. filters.py provides the date range, status, category, sorting, and score range filter controls. metrics_cards.py renders summary metric cards and progress bars. These are the building blocks of the dashboard's triage workflow — all loaded directly by streamlit_app.py.

tour.title: Data Loading Layer
tour.description: app/data_loader.py bridges the dashboard and the database — it has the second-highest fan-out (60 edges) and seventh-highest fan-in (7). It fetches domain data, comments, GitHub repository information, and crawl statistics from Supabase and formats them into Pandas DataFrames for the UI components. Understanding this module is key to following how data flows from the database to the user's screen.

tour.title: Configuration Foundation
tour.description: These two files form the configuration backbone of the entire project. settings.py (fan-in: 9) uses Pydantic BaseSettings to load all application configuration — API tokens, database credentials, LLM endpoints, and UI preferences — from environment variables and .env files. constants.py (fan-in: 13, the highest in the project) defines domain constants: country codes, business models, review statuses, categories, scoring coefficients, and helper dictionaries used across every layer.
tour.languageLesson: Pydantic BaseSettings automatically loads from environment variables and .env files with type coercion. The `model_config` directive enables case-insensitive env var matching. The `frozen` option prevents accidental mutation after instantiation.

tour.title: Database Schema
tour.description: The SQL schema files define the database that backs the entire application. 001_core.sql creates five core tables — domains, crawl_runs, domain_observations, crawl_country_status, domain_comments — with foreign keys, indexes, and audit timestamps. 002_views.sql builds reporting views (v_domains_today, v_domains_this_week, v_crawl_stats) and a paginated RPC function. Later migrations add opportunity-scoring columns and GitHub discovery tables. The schema is the contract between the crawl pipeline and the dashboard.
tour.languageLesson: SQL views encapsulate complex JOIN and aggregation logic behind a simple table-like interface. V_ views (like v_domains_today) join domains with observations and comments, filtering to today's date — the dashboard queries these views rather than raw tables.

tour.title: Database Access Layer
tour.description: Three files implement the database access layer. repositories.py (fan-in: 12, second-highest) provides typed repository classes for domains, observations, crawl runs, comments, and GitHub data — each with Supabase query builder methods. supabase_client.py is a singleton factory for the Supabase client. async_client.py provides thin async HTTP wrappers (GET/POST) for the Supabase REST API. This layer sits between the business logic and the database, handling all CRUD operations.

tour.title: Cloudflare Radar Integration
tour.description: This tightly-coupled cluster (Cluster 1, 12 internal edges) implements the Cloudflare Radar data source. orchestrator.py is the crawl pipeline's central coordinator — it fetches top domains per country via RadarService, deduplicates via dedupe.py, enriches via LMStudioClient, scores via scoring.py, and persists to the database. client.py provides the rate-limited async HTTP client for the Cloudflare API with retry logic for 429/5xx errors. radar_service.py wraps the geo-location and ranking endpoints. schemas.py defines Pydantic models for API responses. progress.py manages crawl run lifecycle — creating, resuming, and tracking completion per country.

tour.title: Domain Deduplication and Scoring
tour.description: These files implement the domain processing rules that run after raw data arrives. normalize.py extracts the registrable domain from URLs using tldextract, handling www/mobile subdomains and international suffixes. dedupe.py orchestrates the dedup pipeline: normalizing domains, upserting new ones, and inserting observations with scores. scoring.py implements the 11-factor trending-founders scoring formula covering rank bonuses, category bonuses, novelty boosts, multi-country presence, LLM potential scaling, known-giant penalties, and already-reviewed penalties — the core algorithm behind domain ranking.
tour.languageLesson: tldextract separates domain labels from TLDs using the Public Suffix List — critical for correctly identifying the registrable domain (e.g., 'example.co.uk' — registrable: 'example', suffix: 'co.uk'). This is essential for deduplication accuracy.

tour.title: LLM Enrichment Pipeline
tour.description: Three files implement the local LLM enrichment system that adds semantic understanding to raw domain rankings. lmstudio_client.py (fan-in: 6, class LMStudioClient has fan-in: 11) is the async client for LM Studio's local inference server with retry logic, JSON schema enforcement, and domain enrichment. prompts.py builds structured prompts combining ranking context, categories, and homepage snippets. schemas.py defines the LLMEnrichmentResult Pydantic model with validated summary, category, business model, and scoring fields. The enrichment runs as part of the daily crawl pipeline.

tour.title: LLM Opportunity Scoring
tour.description: The opportunity scoring subsystem evaluates domains for Romanian market potential. scorer.py wraps the LLM client with a specialized OpportunityScorer class that uses JSON-schema calling and retries on validation failures. update_opportunity_scores.py orchestrates the full pipeline: paginating domains from Supabase, fetching homepage excerpts via HTTP, building domain context with observations and Romanian signals, calling the LLM scorer, applying guardrails (known-giant penalties), and persisting results. A dedicated CLI command drives this pipeline.

tour.title: CI/CD and Operations
tour.description: The crawl.yml GitHub Actions workflow schedules the domain crawler and GitHub opencode crawl five times daily (UTC), with optional skip flags and log artifact upload on failure — this is the production heartbeat of the system. The RUNBOOK.md documents the full operations playbook: quick-start, database setup, pause/resume crawl, daily operations schedule, troubleshooting guides, and database maintenance procedures. Together they show how the system runs autonomously and how operators maintain it.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions cron schedules use five-field cron syntax. This workflow uses `depends_on`-style orchestration: domain crawl runs first, then opportunity scoring, then GitHub opencode crawl. `workflow_dispatch` enables manual triggering with input parameters for testing.
